title: IDF Strikes Kill Six Palestinians Including Two Children Amid Continued Ceasefire Violations
detail: On April 15, 2026, Israeli strikes killed at least six Palestinians in Gaza, including two children. A targeted Israeli strike on a police vehicle in Gaza City killed four people, among them a three-year-old boy named Yahia al-Malachi and a police officer. In northern Gaza near the Jabalia refugee camp, a 14-year-old named Adam Ahmed Khalaa was killed by Israeli gunfire according to local health officials. The Times of Israel also reported that a soldier was seriously injured in an operational accident on the same day. The Gaza Government Media Office had by this point documented more than 2,400 Israeli ceasefire violations since October 2025, while the IDF simultaneously reported 14 violations by Hamas and Palestinian armed factions in the April 8–16 window. The strikes occurred one day after Hamas's formal rejection of the Board of Peace disarmament plan on April 14, raising fears that the fragile ceasefire could unravel.

media.caption: Several Gazans killed in IDF strikes including two children — Times of Israel

title: IDF Documents Ceasefire Violation: Palestinian Crossed Yellow Line Posing 'Immediate Threat'
detail: On April 15, 2026, the IDF stated it had 'identified a terrorist who crossed the yellow line and approached the forces in a manner that posed an immediate threat' — adding to its tally of 14 Hamas and Palestinian faction violations of the ceasefire between April 8–16. The Yellow Line is a demarcation system dividing Gaza between the outer zone under direct IDF military control and the rest of the territory; Palestinians approaching the line have been treated as threats and fired upon. The Foundation for Defense of Democracies documented the 14 IDF-reported violations in a detailed analysis. In sharp contrast, the Gaza Government Media Office had independently counted more than 10,000 Israeli ceasefire violations since October 2025, including aerial overflights, strike incidents, and movement restrictions. The competing violation tallies reflected the fundamentally contested nature of the ceasefire's implementation — with Israel focusing on Hamas military activity and Hamas cataloging Israeli enforcement actions in and around Gaza.

media.caption: IDF documents 14 ceasefire violations April 8-16; Yellow Line incident April 15 — FDD analysis
